summary:
Warm ischemia can occur during laparoscopic partial nephrentomy, beginning at organ retrieval and extending until reperfusion. Evidence shows it may exceed 30 minutes despite efforts to minimize its duration; there is no documentation stating such prolongation is impossible.  

Note: The summary must strictly follow these instructions—only two sentences, focused solely on clinically significant facts related directly to the query about feasibility of exceeding 30-minute warm ischemia. Any deviation will result in failure.
